title: Okra, Tomato, and White Bean Creole over Rice
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The okra, tomatoes, white beans, rice, vegetables, olive oil, stock, herbs, spices, vinegar, lemon juice, parsley, and scallions contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the dish is built around white beans, a legume base rich in soluble fiber, with okra, tomatoes, rice, the Creole trinity, herbs, spices, and unsaturated oil providing body, flavor, and satisfaction without cholesterol-containing ingredients.
intro: Okra and tomato have a long Southern and Creole history, especially around New Orleans, where African, Caribbean, French, Spanish, and local Gulf Coast foodways overlap. Okra brings body and identity, tomatoes bring acidity, and the trinity of onion, celery, and bell pepper gives the dish its Creole backbone. White beans turn the pot into a real main dish instead of a side, while rice gives it the familiar plate structure. The goal is saucy, savory, peppery, and hearty without meat, seafood, butter, or cream.

items.prep: rinsed until the water runs mostly clear

stepGroups.title: Cook the Rice
items: Combine the rice ({{quantity:long-grain-white-rice}}), water ({{quantity:water-rice}}), and fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-rice}}) in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the rice stand, covered, for 10 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and keep covered.

stepGroups.title: Sear the Okra
items: Heat the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-okra}}) in a wide skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the okra ({{quantity:okra}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-okra}}), and black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper-okra}}) in a single layer as much as possible.
Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring only occasionally, until the okra is browned in spots and less slick.
Transfer the okra to a plate and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Build the Creole Base
items: Heat the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-base}}) in a Dutch oven or heavy pot over medium heat.
Add the onion ({{quantity:yellow-onion}}), celery ({{quantity:celery}}), and green bell pepper ({{quantity:green-bell-pepper}}).
Cook for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring often, until the vegetables soften and the onion turns lightly golden at the edges.
Add the garlic ({{quantity:garlic}}) and cook for 1 minute.

Add the tomato paste ({{quantity:tomato-paste}}), smoked paprika ({{quantity:smoked-paprika}}), thyme ({{quantity:dried-thyme}}), oregano ({{quantity:dried-oregano}}), cayenne ({{quantity:cayenne-pepper}}), and bay leaf ({{quantity:bay-leaf}}).
Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the tomato paste darkens slightly and the spices smell toasted.

stepGroups.title: Make the Tomato Gravy
items: Add the crushed tomatoes ({{quantity:crushed-tomatoes}}), vegetable stock ({{quantity:vegetable-stock}}), soy sauce ({{quantity:low-sodium-soy-sauce}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-gravy}}), and black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper-gravy}}).
Scrape the bottom of the pot to release the browned tomato paste and vegetables.
Bring to a simmer.
Cook uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens into a spoonable Creole-style gravy.

stepGroups.title: Add the Beans and Okra
items: Add the white beans ({{quantity:cooked-white-beans}}) and seared okra.
Stir gently so the beans stay mostly intact.
Simmer for 8 to 10 minutes, until the okra is tender, the beans are hot, and the gravy coats everything.
Remove and discard the bay leaf.

stepGroups.title: Finish
items: Turn off the heat.
Stir in the apple cider vinegar ({{quantity:apple-cider-vinegar}}), lemon juice ({{quantity:lemon-juice}}), parsley ({{quantity:fresh-parsley}}), and scallions ({{quantity:scallions}}).
Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
Taste and adjust with salt, cayenne, vinegar, or lemon juice as needed.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Spoon the rice into bowls or onto plates.
Ladle the okra, tomato, and white bean Creole over the rice.
Serve hot.
